entry.title: Why sustainability is both a responsibility and an opportunity for a Hospital ?
entry.content:
For hospitals, sustainability is a core duty - patient care and sustainability must go hand in hand, because healing that harms is self-defeating.

Hospitals are places of healing. They exist to protect life, restore health, and support people during their most vulnerable moments. But what if the systems designed to care for us are quietly harming the very conditions that make health possible in the first place?
Consider this. The electricity used by a single hospital bed over the course of one year is roughly equivalent to the annual energy consumption of four row houses. Or take anesthesia. Just one hour using Desflurane can generate emissions comparable to driving 375 to 750 kilometers by car. These examples make one thing clear. Hospitals are essential to our wellbeing, and they also have a significant environmental footprint

Globally, the healthcare sector accounts for around 4.4 percent of total CO₂ emissions – if Healthcare would be a country it would be the 6th biggest emitter and more than double of international shipping. Hospitals contribute a large share of this impact due to their continuous operation and complex infrastructure. Heating and cooling systems run around the clock. Medical devices, pharmaceuticals, catering services, buildings and logistics all require large amounts of energy and resources. Water use alone is striking. In Germany, every single hospital bed consumes more than 200 thousand liters of water per year, which equals around 500 liters per day. Modern healthcare saves lives, and it is also highly resource intensive.

At the same time, environmental degradation directly affects human health. Climate change, air pollution and water scarcity increase the prevalence of allergies and infections and place additional strain on already burdened healthcare systems. Hospitals therefore face a dual responsibility. Caring for patients today and protecting the environmental conditions that make health possible tomorrow.

From insights to actions: how hospitals shape a sustainable future

One of the biggest challenges are insights. Many hospitals understand that sustainability matters but struggle to realize where their environmental impact actually arises. Emissions and resource usage are spread across everyday operations such as energy use, medical products, supply chains, infrastructure, waste, and catering. Without transparency, sustainability efforts remain fragmented, focus areas follow gut-feelings and opportunities for meaningful improvement are easily missed.

Sustainability becomes powerful when it is part of everyday decisions. Process- and data-driven insights help hospitals understand how care delivery, resource use, and responsibility are intertwined.

They make environmental impact visible across daily operations. This helps hospitals distinguish between what cannot be changed and where smarter use of resources is possible. A hospital bed must be cleaned after every patient, and hygiene standards are non-negotiable. What can change is how water is used across the hospital as a whole once consumption patterns and inefficiencies become visible.

Sustainability becomes a question of understanding and action rather than restriction.

  • Sustainability supports economic responsibility. When medical products are purchased, hospitals can understand how price, availability, and supply chains relate to one another. This creates transparency about whether a product’s environmental impact is in proportion to its economic value and clinical necessity. Sustainability becomes part of sound purchasing decisions rather than a trade off against efficiency.
  • Social responsibility is another essential part of this picture. Hospitals must function at all times, even during disruptions, crisis, or power outages. In operating rooms or intensive care units, power loss is not an option. Decisions about backup systems, energy independence and emergency preparedness directly affect patient safety. Digital insights help hospitals understand critical dependencies, assess risks and plan for resilience. This supports safer working conditions for staff and greater security for patients who depend on uninterrupted care.

 

Sustainability must become accessible to everyone and pa rt of everyday decisions. Reports and insights available without complex analyses  keep teams informed and enable decisions – AI powered -  based on reliable and current data.

Sustainability in healthcare is not about perfection. It is about awareness, responsibility and progress. It is about protecting health today and preserving the conditions for health tomorrow – act now.

Sustainability is what remains when we leave - Let us make sure healthcare leaves something that truly lasts.
Visit us on Sustainability Software, Solutions & Services | SAP and/or reach out to us or your SAP account manager to find out how SAP can support you in fulfilling your sustainability ambitions and provide exceptional care for your patients.

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Business Data Cloud Express Academy
entry.content:
If you’re an SAP Partner who keeps hearing “SAP Business Data Cloud” in every strategy meeting and thinks, I really need to get ahead of this, this academy is for you.

Our Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for SAP Business Data Cloud brings SAP experts and partners together to demystify:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Business Data Cloud

We’ll walk through real-world flows, not just slides, how data moves, how models are built, how insights land in the hands of business users.  Plus, you’ll prove your skills with a hands-on test in a live system before you even book the certification exam.

The goal?  You walk away with the fundamentals and core concepts to feel genuinely ready for the SAP Certified - SAP Business Data Cloud exam.  You just need basic data & analytics knowledge and the willingness to lean in and learn.

Honestly, if you want to be “the cloud data go-to” in your team, this is a smart place to start.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010285.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessDataCloud #SAPSkills #DataAndAnalytics #SAPTraining

entry.title: Unlock Enterprise-Scale Integration and Automation with SAP: A 5-Part Webcast Series
entry.content:
Running integration at enterprise scale requires more than connecting systems - it demands a unified strategy, consistent governance, and a strong operational foundation. To help organizations navigate this complex landscape, we’re excited to announce a deep-dive 5-part webcast series that shows how SAP Integration Suite and SAP Business Technology Platform can serve as a true enterprise-wide integration platform.

Whether you’re looking to simplify complexity, strengthen governance, or enable future-ready automation, this series provides a structured path from vision to sustainable execution. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect from each session:

 

1. Rethinking Integration: Building a Unified Enterprise-wide Platform

Integration starts with consolidation and unification. This session explores how organizations can position SAP Integration Suite as a central platform, connecting systems, processes, and data across the enterprise. Learn strategies to streamline your integration landscape and create a foundation for scalable, enterprise-wide operations.

Register here:  https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661068&sourcepage=register

 

2. From Vision to Execution: Building a Scalable Integration Strategy

Turning integration vision into reality requires a deliberate, structured approach. This session dives into the steps organizations can take to develop a scalable integration strategy that aligns business goals with technology execution. Discover best practices for governance, architecture, and execution that drive measurable value.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661111&sourcepage=register

 

3. Achieve Enterprise-grade Operations for SAP Integration Suite

Operational excellence is critical to sustaining integration at scale. In this session, we focus on how to strengthen governance, monitor performance, and build resilient processes with SAP Integration Suite. Learn how enterprise-grade operations ensure consistency, reliability, and scalability across your integration landscape.

Regiser here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259511&sessionid=1&key=905F0343E0BA503A283C97B3645BCB31&groupId=6667225&sourcepage=register

 

4. Drive Intelligent Business Transformation with Agentic Enterprise Automation

Intelligent business transformation requires more than isolated automation - it demands connected, adaptive processes powered by AI. Explore how SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Transformation Management enable agentic enterprise automation at scale. Learn how to design, govern, and measure AI agents that drive true process transformation, generating measurable outcomes across complex enterprise environments.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259684&sessionid=1&key=10935CA2537B96624DD065F7EA514C2F&groupId=6661382&sourcepage=register

 

5. Develop Robust Automations with SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite

Agentic enterprise automation only succeeds when systems, workflows, and applications are seamlessly connected. This session demonstrates how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite work together to enable end-to-end automation across SAP and non-SAP landscapes. See live demos, customer success stories, and practical examples showing how low-code development and enterprise-grade integration accelerate automation while maintaining control, visibility, and scalability.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5258498&sessionid=1&key=76AD4CD903C206A20A2CFFE8270CC25C&groupId=6661286&sourcepage=register

 

This series is designed for integration architects, IT leaders, and business transformation professionals who want to take their enterprise integration and automation initiatives to the next level. Join us to learn how SAP can help you simplify complexity, achieve operational excellence, and unlock intelligent, future-ready automation.

Save your spot 🙂

entry.title: SAP MDG News: AI Use Cases delivered, demoed and presented
entry.content:
Last week, I had the opportunity to present the latest SAP MDG and AI developments to a major client. Since the 2023 release, SAP MDG has unlocked numerous AI use cases, with most integrated directly into Joule and additional embedded AI features available. The live demo showcased all these capabilities in action.

Key AI Use Cases Overview

We're currently seeing three interaction types of Joule with MDG that are transforming how users work with master data:

Use Case 1: Informational

Knowledge at Your Fingertips: With Joule integrated into the SAP Launchpad, business users can ask MDG-related questions without leaving their current workspace. Instead of opening external browsers or applications, users can simply ask questions like "Does MDG support parallel processes for the same object?" Joule accesses the complete SAP Help documentation and SAP Community to deliver high-quality answers instantly.

Use Case 2: Navigational

Smart Data Discovery: Users can pose specific questions about master data within their MDG system. For example, asking "Show me all Business Partners with NWT in their name" displays results directly within the Joule chatbot. Users can then navigate directly from Joule to the appropriate app or action, eliminating the traditional process of opening the correct app, searching for business partners, and then proceeding with tasks.

 

Use Case 3: Transactional

Guided Process Creation: This powerful capability is already available in MDG cloud ready mode. When creating a new Business Partner via Joule, users interact within the chatbot interface, receiving guided assistance with the right questions for BP creation. The system also provides duplicate detection alerts directly within the chat experience

Custom AI Solutions Discussion

Beyond standard use cases, we explored customer-specific implementations using SAP Business AI to enhance master data quality. The concept of an "Attribute Recommendation Agent" could automatically improve data quality through regular evaluation and remediation processes, keeping master data current and accurate through automated quality checks.

Client Feedback Section

The feedback on delivered use cases was very positive! We also discussed exciting possibilities for implementing custom AI use cases with SAP Business AI to further enhance their master data governance processes.

Call to Action

For more detailed insights, check out this comprehensive blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/meet-joule-your-ai-copilot-for-sap-master-data-governance/ba-p/14273811

Meet Joule - Your AI Copilot for SAP Master Data Governance 🤖

Best regards,

Steffen

entry.title: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – March 2026
entry.content:
We’re kicking off 2026 with a new set of innovations in SAP HANA Cloud, continuing our evolution as the database that AI was looking for.

The Q1 2026 release introduces capabilities that enhance automation, flexibility, and intelligent data interaction, from infrastructure expansion and improved scalability to new agent-driven approaches for exploring enterprise data.  Together, these innovations help organizations reduce complexity, optimize performance, and unlock faster insights.

Let’s dive into some of the key highlights of this release.


SAP HANA Cloud agents

Following our announcement at TechEd 2025, we are excited to bring you the Data & Discovery Agent Procedures in SAP HANA Cloud.

As part of our journey toward a more agentic and AI-native data platform, these agent procedures combine knowledge graph–based discovery with AI-driven query generation. This allows users to engage with their data in a more intuitive, conversational way, reducing the need for deep SQL expertise.

With the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents, business users and developers alike can more easily bridge the gap between business questions and complex data models, accelerating access to insights and enabling more efficient data exploration.

To support early adoption and gather valuable feedback, this capability is now available through a Beta Program, with registration starting April 1. We invite you to participate, explore the features firsthand, and help shape the future of agent-driven data interaction in SAP HANA Cloud.

Register here for the Beta Program:
https://url.sap/399tsd 

 

For more information, check out this blogpost by @shabana 

 

Machine Learning and NLP services Updates 

With the Q1 2026 release of SAP HANA Cloud, we continue to strengthen embedded Machine Learning and AI capabilities across the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), and the Natural Language Processing (NLP) services. These enhancements focus on making tabular AI predictions using custom AI models or semantic data retrieval scenario easier to use and applied to real business application data. 

Key highlights of this release are: 

  • A new Text column TFIDF with truncatedSVD transformer operator in AutoML, for simplest and efficient text feature processing for use cases like Service Ticket Intelligence 
  • A new dimension reduction function for stored text embedding vectors, allowing to reduce high cardinality vectors to lower cardinality of 62, 128 or 256 dimensions for faster processing while retaining key semantic insights 
  • A new feature space reduction function Feature Agglomeration, allowing to group correlated feature columns into compact, interpretable clusters, thus reducing feature dimensionality 
  • Support for clustering of Vector columns using the Affinity Propagation clustering function 
  • Faster text classification function, supporting float precision for better performance 
  • Introducing a SQL CROSS_ENCODE function, for more precise semantic re-ranking of search retrieval results using the SAP HANA Cloud NLP services  
  • The python Machine Learning client for SAP HANA 2.28 supporting several new functions like text log parsing, cross encoder and parameter optimization with the multi-task multilayer perception neural network function. 

 

 

 

Calculation View Modeling 

As with every release, numerous new capabilities and enhancements were added to Calculation View Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. One of the highlights clearly is the ability to semantically import SAP HANA Cloud Calculation Views into SAP Business Data Cloud, for reuse purpose without data duplication.

To learn more about additional Calculation View Modeling innovations, check out this blogpost by JanZwickel.

Reuse Calculation Views in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 

Combined, SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud are a strong foundation for your data and analytics use cases.
Semantic onboarding in SAP Business Data Cloud enables calculation views from SAP HANA Cloud to be leveraged in BDC without transferring data, only semantics are transferred, creating BDC proxies.
As such, we enable uses to re-use existing investments made into calculation views, in SAP BDC. Moreover, we foster a HANA-native approach of modeling, tightly integrated with BDC capabilities; enabling you to extend our HANA-based data marts with SAP BDC. Untimely, allowing you to benefit from both solutions in combination.

AT the moment, two integration modes are available: 

  • Flat View: Onboards views as flat structures (not for Dimension Views). Use for minimal BDC enhancements. 
  • Construction Kit: Imports only private attributes, allowing flexible recombination in BDC. Supports Dimension Views. 

Onboarded semantics include input parameters, variables, currency/unit conversion, labels, hierarchies, and information about star-join dimensions. Further enhancements are planned, and roadmap is driven by customer feedback.

Are you eager to adopt the bi-directional integration between SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud? If yes, learn more about our SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program down below.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program

Calculation Views in the Context of SAP Business Data Cloud

To further support customers and partners exploring integration scenarios between SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud, we are introducing a dedicated SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program focused Calculation Views in the Context of SAP Business Data Cloud

This program is designed to help participants explore how existing SAP HANA Cloud calculation views can be leveraged in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud, particularly through semantic onboarding, which enables reuse of models without requiring data replication. In addition, participants can evaluate scenarios such as building new calculation views on top of SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products.

Through this initiative, we aim to work closely with customers and partners who are actively working on, or planning, projects in this space. Participants will have the opportunity to gain early insights into new capabilities, receive guidance during implementation, and provide direct feedback that helps shape future enhancements.

Participation in the program is limited and based on alignment with the program scope and project requirements.

If you are interested in joining the Customer Care Program, stay tuned to this blogpost and our #whatsnewinsaphanacloud hashtag as we will open the registration soon!

 

SQL Tools for Administration in SAP HANA Cloud Central 

SQL Console Code Completion 

With the Q1 Release, the SQL console in HANA Cloud Central now includes enhanced code completion that suggests both SQL snippets and schema objects as you type, making it quicker and easier to work with SQL. As users begin typing, the console will display recommended snippets such as CREATE TABLE, CREATE PROCUDURE, or GRANT. When the user selects a statement, it will insert a complete template automatically. It also provides lookups for tables, views, and columns within the current schema, reducing errors, and helping users find the objects they need. These improvements boost productivity for experienced users and make the SQL experience far more approachable for those who are new to SQL. 

 

SQL Console GenAI 

The SQL Console now includes new GenAI‑powered actions that make working with SQL faster and more accessible. Within the Run menu, users will find three new options: Run Prompt, Explain SQL Statement using AI, and Optimize SQL Statement using AI.  

By selecting text in the SQL Console and choosing Run Prompt, users can generate SQL statements directly from natural‑language instructions. The Explain option provides clear natural‑language explanations of SQL statements, including complex queries, while Optimize SQL Statement uses AI to refine and improve existing SQL for better performance. These enhancements help users work more efficiently and make SQL much easier for those who are new to it. 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Clients 

Addition of the SAP HANA database client to the package manager for .NET 

The SAP HANA database client is officially available on NuGet, making it easier than ever for developers to integrate SAP HANA Cloud into their applications. By adding the SAP-provided packages directly from the SAP profile and NuGet, developers can quickly include both the HANA .NET driver and Entity Framework Core provider without needing manual downloads or custom setup. Users can also now see the updated tutorials on Connecting Using the Microsoft Entity Framework Core (EF Core) and SAP HANA .NET Interface. 

Direct connection option for replicas 

The SAP HANA Client supports a direct connection option for replicas, enabling applications to explicitly route read-only workloads away from the source system. Developers can now use a SQL hint such as WITH HINT (RESULT_LAG('hana_sr')) to target replica nodes for read‑only queries or specify the connection property replicationRole=REPLICA to ensure the connection is established directly with a replica. This capability improves cost efficiency and performance by offloading analytical workloads from the source system while still maintaining full high‑availability readiness. We also have this new tutorial on Routing queries to a read only replica. 

 

New installation program for the data lake client  

The data lake client now includes a new installation program based on the SAP HANA database lifecycle manager, providing a more unified and streamlined setup experience. This installer will be available through the SAP Software Center and supports Microsoft Windows, Linux x64, and Linux ARM64. When downloaded from the SAP Software Center, it also includes the required cryptographic libraries, simplifying deployment, and ensuring secure operation. This harmonized installation process makes it easier for customers to install and manage clients for SAP HANA Cloud. 

 

Enabling HTTP proxy support for the data lake client 

The data lake client now supports connecting through an HTTP proxy, enabling better compatibility with enterprise network environments. Users can enable proxy usage by configuring the settings proxyHTTP=true, along with proxyHost, proxyPort, proxyUser, and proxyPassword. These options allow the client to route traffic through an organization’s proxy infrastructure while maintaining secure and controlled access. This enhancement ensures the data lake client fits seamlessly into enterprise deployments that rely on HTTP proxy requirements. 

 

Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central

With the Q1 2026 release, SAP HANA Cloud Central introduces the Performance Analyzer, a new capability for analyzing historical performance data of SAP HANA Cloud database instances.

The Performance Analyzer provides access to up to 42 days of historical metrics, enabling administrators to identify performance issues, investigate root causes, and correlate system behavior over time. This complements real-time monitoring by adding the historical visibility required for effective troubleshooting.

Key capabilities include:

  • Load History Chart
    A central entry point for analysis, allowing users to identify anomalies, select time ranges, and correlate performance changes with system events across a 42-day history.
  • Chart Library
    A collection of predefined visualizations across categories such as CPU, memory, locks, transactions, and workload, which can be added dynamically to analysis views.
  • Preset and Custom Views
    Predefined views for common scenarios such as CPU, memory, and lock analysis, with the ability to create and save custom views tailored to specific needs.
  • Interactive exploration
    Support for tooltips, synchronized time selection, and zooming, enabling detailed analysis of specific time periods.

The Performance Analyzer is integrated across SAP HANA Cloud Central and can be accessed through multiple entry points, including the side navigation, instance overview, alerts, command palette, and Joule.

Typical use cases include analyzing CPU utilization, investigating memory consumption, and troubleshooting performance degradation by comparing historical system behavior.

To learn more, please check out this blogpost by @Hai 

 

Data Integration 

Smart Data Access: Support for a New Cloud MySQL Remote Source Through the Data Access Agent 

With the Smart Data Access (SDA) Data Access Agent introduced in QRC 03/2025, readonly access to a new remote source, MySQL in the cloud, will be available starting in QRC 01/2026. 

Configuration and usage remain the same as for previously supported remote sources. For newly provisioned Data Access Agents, all new adapters are activated by default. To activate new adapters on an already provisioned Data Access Agent, refer to SAP Note 2600176, SAP HANA Smart Data Access Supported Remote Sources, for details on activation and support information. 

 

Elasticity 

Elastic Compute Node: Automated ECN Provisioning and Deprovisioning Through ECN Policy 

SAP HANA Cloud introduces a significant enhancement to Elastic Compute Node management with the new ECN policy feature, available starting with QRC 01/2026. Previously, ECN Advisor recommendations required manual action. With ECN policies, the system can now automatically provision and deprovision ECNs based on your workload patterns. After running the ECN Advisor, you can choose scheduled scaling for predictable usage or demand based scaling for more dynamic environments. 

Scheduled scaling allows you to define recurring timebased rules to add or remove ECNs. Demand based scaling adjusts capacity automatically according to system load, and the initial release provides two scaling focuses, Availability and Cost. These options determine how aggressively the system reacts to load changes, with the ability to scale up to sixteen ECNs sequentially. Table replication continues to require separate handling, and each instance supports one demand based policy with the option to configure multiple scheduled policies. More details can be found in the About ECN Policies section. 

 

Further news:

SAP HANA Cloud Datacenter expansion 

In Q1 2026, we are expanding the global availability of SAP HANA Cloud in four new BTP regions: 

  • Germany (Frankfurt) and UK (London) on Microsoft Azure, 
  • Japan (Tokyo) and US East (Sterling) on SAP Cloud Infrastructure (SCI). 

With these additions, SAP HANA Cloud is now available in more than 40 public cloud locations worldwide. While most regions run on hyperscalers, SAP HANA Cloud continues to strengthen its footprint on SAP Cloud Infrastructure (SCI), which now spans seven regions (see Figure 1). This expansion provides customers with greater flexibility to choose both the infrastructure provider and deployment region that best align with their business, performance, and regulatory requirements. 

EU22—the second Microsoft Azure region in the EU—lays the foundation for future multi-region disaster recovery within the EU. Similarly, US01—the second SCI presence in the United States—prepares the groundwork for future multi-region disaster recovery capabilities in the US. For the latest updates on the availability timeline for multi-region disaster recovery in these regions, please refer to SAP Roadmap Explorer. 

SAP HANA Cloud Public Cloud Locations  

 

Expanded Performance Class Options Across Hyperscalers

SAP HANA Cloud continues to enhance flexibility in sizing and performance across supported infrastructures.

On Alibaba Cloud, SAP HANA database instances now support additional performance classes and instance sizes. With the QRC 01/2026 update, you can provision configurations tailored to different workload requirements, ranging from memory-intensive scenarios with up to 8 TB of memory to more compute-focused setups. This provides greater flexibility to align system sizing with specific application needs.
More details about the available options can be found SAP HANA Database Size.  

On AWS, a new 16 GB instance size is now available, designed specifically for low-throughput and lightweight workloads. This option enables more efficient deployment of smaller applications, development and testing environments, and cost-sensitive use cases, helping avoid overprovisioning.

This instance size will also be made available on Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure, further extending consistent sizing flexibility across all hyperscalers supported by SAP HANA Cloud.

 

 

Thanks for taking the time to explore the What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud on Q1 2026 innovations!

If you’d like to go deeper into the technical details of the March 2026 release, we recommend visiting the What’s New Viewer in the technical documentation. It provides a comprehensive overview of the full release scope. To stay informed about upcoming innovations, announcements, and best practices, follow the SAP HANA Cloud tag.

You can also find our latest blog posts by searching for the #whatsnewinsaphanacloud hashtag.

If you missed earlier What’s New webinars, you’ll find recordings of past sessions and upcoming events in our YouTube playlist. Our What’s New webinar covering the Q1 2026 innovations will be posted on this playlist very soon.

Do you have questions about SAP HANA Cloud or want to share your thoughts on the new features?
Join the conversation in the SAP HANA Cloud Community Q&A or leave a comment below. We’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: Positioning SAP BTP as part of SAP Business Suite certification
entry.content:
If you’ve ever paused mid-customer conversation thinking, “I know SAP BTP is the answer here, but how do I explain it clearly”?  This webinar is for you.

entry.title: Demystifying Model Context Protocol (MCP), Part 1: The Foundation
entry.content:
Introduction

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming the standard way for AI agents to talk to APIs and data sources. Today, many projects wire LLMs to backends with custom, one‑off glue code that is hard to reuse or share.

MCP changes this by defining a common language for tools and resources that any compatible agent can understand with the help of LLMs.

In this three‑part series:

  1. In Part-1, we will look at what MCP is, why it matters and when it is worth adopting in your landscape.
  2. In Part 2, we will build and deploy a commerce MCP server on SAP BTP and
  3. In Part 3, we will connect that MCP server to Joule Agent Builder to power real agentic scenarios.

What is MCP?

By now, everyone knows the full form of MCP - Model Context Protocol. Let us understand some more details and building blocks which makes MCP talk of the town nowadays.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard through which AI applications connect to external enterprise systems,  APIs, databases and object stores. MCP wraps the complexity of heterogeneous systems and abstracts integrations away from the Agentic solutions so that agentic solutions can focus of AI piece and spend less time on integrations.

Metaphor:  Think of MCP as a universal electric adapter. Every country has its own outlet shape just as every system exposes its own API style. With MCP,  Agentic AI applications only needs one adapter. MCP defines a consistent way to describe tools and resources to plug into many different systems without integrating each one from scratch.

Building blocks of MCP

  1. Host Application + MCP Client: This is basically your agentic AI solution which embeds MCP client that talks to the MCP server on behalf of the LLM.  MCP client discovers available tools exposed by the MCP server and routes the LLM's request for data to the MCP server.
  2. MCP Server: MCP server is the application or a program which exposes the capabilities of your application or enterprise system to the host application through MCP standards.
  3. Tools: Tools are actions that the MCP server can perform. For example: "Get Product List", "Create Cart", "Add Product to Cart" etc. Tools gives host applications a structured set of capabilities.
  4. Transport Layer: MCP uses standard communication like STDIO for local and HTTP with Server‑Sent Events for remote servers.

The diagram below gives a overview of how MCP acts as a facade for the agentic AI solutions.

Figure-1

Now as we understand the "What", let us jump into "Why".

Why MCP matter?

In this section, I have listed down 3 main advantages MCP brings on the table

  1. Reduce custom integrations and increase reusability: Without MCP every AI application would build it's own integration and adapters to talk to heterogeneous APIs. MCP standardizes how tools and APIs are exposed.
  2. Provide access to data: LLMs require data to provide valuable outputs. Agents can provide this data to LLMs using MCP tools. This helps to increase the accuracy of the agentic solution and reduce hallucinations.
  3. Better managed security: MCP servers act like a facade to your enterprise data and resources. You can think of centralizing the authentication, authorization and audit logging for the underlying systems.
  4. Scale AI solutions: As MCP servers mature, you can add more tools which indirectly adds capabilities to your host application.

When it's worth to adopt MCP?

By now, you have got a good idea of MCP, but should we start building MCP server for every agentic solution? This section outlines some pointers - when MCP makes sense.

  1. When you need one integration used by many agents in your enterprise.
  2. When you want to access heterogeneous systems in a consistent way.
  3. When your agentic solution depends on real enterprise data
  4. When your agentic AI landscape is likely to grow and you do not want to reinvent integrations.

Brainstorming the need of MCP server is very important otherwise it can be one more additional code base for the team to maintain and scale.

Wrap-up

At this point, we have looked at 

  • What is Model Context Protocol.
  • The problem it solves in ever growing agentic world.
  • Building blocks of MCP
  • When it is worth adopting MCP in your enterprise landscape.

In Part-2 of the series, we will move from theory to code. I will build a MCP server, run it locally and then deploy it on SAP BTP and we can see how it can power real agentic use cases.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol
  2. https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/learn/architecture

entry.title: How to Integrate ABAP Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Overview

If you are integrating your Fiori apps from SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP Build Work Zone, you may face the challenge of exposing derived and composite roles containing launchpad content.

Creating (PFCG) roles on ABAP system, that derive from roles with launchpad content, is mainly driven by the need to differentiate authorization for the same set of applications. This often leads to many roles deriving from the same base role. In such cases, the derived roles are assigned to users instead of their base roles. The derived roles get the content of the role menu, like Fiori catalogs or spaces from inheritance and focus mainly on authorizations and user assignments.

This blog outlines the approach for exposing derived roles from an ABAP system to SAP Build Work Zone.

Related Blog

In the blog How to Integrate ABAP Composite Roles in SAP Build Work Zone you will learn two options when exposing composite roles. The recommended approach for integrating derived roles is the same as outlined in “Option 2: Indirect Exposure via Single Roles (with IPS)” when exposing composite roles.

Integrating Derived Roles in SAP Build Work Zone

When exposing derived roles to SAP Build Work Zone there is a separation of concerns between the exposure of launchpad content, authorization and role-to-user assignment.

For the exposure, you select only the single roles that contain launchpad content in /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE. Derived roles are not supported in the exposure report. This restriction prevents exposing a high number of roles with identical launchpad content. When the content for exposure is processed, the system identifies roles which derive from the selected roles and includes these references in the exposed CDM content (as relatedRoleAssignments). These references are not exposed to the administrator during exposure but used by the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) and SAP Build Work Zone infrastructure.

 

Identity Provisioning Service and SAP Build Work Zone handle the role assignment logic, ensuring that users with derived roles on the ABAP side get the correct roles assigned on the BTP side. With that, users get access to the application they are authorized for.

Key points

  • Each exposed single role includes information about roles deriving from it and their relationships, but those derived roles are not directly available in the Content Manager on BTP.
  • The IPS ensures that when an user is assigned to a derived role in ABAP, all required single roles (now exposed) are assigned in Work Zone/BTP. For further information, see  Configure Integration with the Identity Provisioning Service and Exploring Role Assignments
  • This approach is supported since SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition release 2022 and later.

 

Conclusion 

Integrating derived roles with SAP Build Work Zone works best with IPS-driven provisioning, as it preserves existing role structure and automated user to role assignment. Without IPS one must manually ensure that corresponding roles in both BTP and ABAP system are assigned, which is impractical in larger scenarios. The IPS based approach also supports the integration of composite roles, which are often used together with derived roles.  In that case only single roles should be exposed. Information about related derived or composite roles is provided though CDM content and processed by SAP Build Work Zone and IPS.

 

Further Information

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Restrictions

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council-Sales: Driving Better Collaboration and Product Usability
entry.content:
SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council
Making collaboration more direct. Making products more usable.

As more customers adopt SAP Public Cloud, partners play an increasingly critical role in sales, delivery, and customer success.

At the same time, we have consistently heard feedback from our partners:

  • The need for earlier visibility into SAP’s roadmap
  • Better clarity on what capabilities will be delivered and when
  • A more direct way to bring real project requirements into product discussions

To address these needs, SAP Public Cloud Sales has launched the SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council (PAC).

Why PAC?

Traditionally, partner feedback has been collected through channels such as WCR and CIP.

While these mechanisms are important for structured feedback, we have observed some limitations:

  • Feedback cycles can be relatively long, making it difficult to respond quickly to real project needs
  • Inputs are often based on individual requirements rather than broader business scenarios
  • The process of how feedback influences the product roadmap is not always transparent

PAC is designed to complement these channels by providing a more direct and interactive way to collaborate.

Our goal is simple: enable faster communication, clearer alignment, and improved product usability.

What we do in PAC

PAC focuses on real product topics and real project challenges.

Roadmap sharing
We share the latest roadmap updates for SAP Public Cloud (e.g. S/4HANA Cloud, BTP), including planned and under-evaluation capabilities, and explain their impact on sales and project delivery.

Real-time discussion and Q&A
We collect partner questions in advance, discuss key topics during the session, and define clear follow-up actions where needed.

Direct connection with product teams
PAC enables direct interaction between partners and product teams, helping ensure that real business scenarios are better reflected in product design.

Real use cases and experience sharing
Partners are encouraged to share project experiences, integration and extensibility scenarios, as well as sales and delivery insights.

What we have discussed so far

PAC sessions are built around real agendas.

Topics from previous sessions include:

  • SAP Cloud ERP for Sales strategy and roadmap
  • Fiori roadmap (e.g. 2602 / 2608) with system demos
  • Manage Sales Documents scenarios
  • Extensibility and UI use cases
  • Custom Sales Order Type
  • Partner Success Stories and Customer Stories
  • Product surveys, user research, and partner feedback

Each session also includes dedicated time for open discussion, Q&A, and clear next steps.

A continuously evolving format

PAC is not a one-time event.

We continuously shape future PAC sessions based on the topics partners are most interested in. This ensures that discussions remain relevant and that feedback can effectively influence product improvements.

What partners gain

By participating in PAC, partners can:

  • Better understand product direction and roadmap
  • Reduce uncertainty in sales and project delivery
  • Bring requirements more directly into product discussions
  • Learn from other partners’ real-world experiences

How to join

The SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council is invitation-based.

To ensure open and productive discussions, participating partners are required to sign an SAP FBA (Feedback Agreement).

If you are interested in joining PAC, please contact:

Bernie.xiong@sap.com

We will follow up with further details and invitations.

entry.title: SAP Developer News for March 26th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Bye Bye Build Apps

  • Deprecation Announcement (blog post): https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-build-apps-deprecation-and-the-path-forward/ba-p/14351750
  • The Path Forward, or what you can do now (documentation): https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-apps-path-forward?locale=en-US&q=sap+build+apps

Edge Integration Cell updates

  • What's new in Edge Integration Cell: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?locale=en-US&lastSent=2026-03-23&Component=Edge+Integration+Cell

A2A Editor

  • A2A Editor: https://open-resource-discovery.github.io/a2a-editor/
  • CodeJam Roadshow Brazil: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2026-brazil-edition-kicking-off-in-march-and/ba-p/14317926

DYK  #9 – Orchestration Client Configuration

  • More information: https://help.sap.com/docs/ai-launchpad/sap-ai-launchpad/orchestration-4953dc10c6dd48fe85f37b41109dffe2?q=orchestration+workflow+ai

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Build Apps Retirement

2:42 Edge Integration Cell updates

3:31 A2A Editor

4:58 DYK #9 Orchestration client Configuration

Transcription

00;00;00;17 - 00;00;06;15
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for March, the 26, 2026.

00;00;06;18 - 00;00;09;26
Daniel Wroblewski
I’m recording this in the bomb shelter of our apartment building to give

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;38;06
Daniel Wroblewski
you some bad news. SAP Build Apps has been deprecated. Now, it all started in 2021 when SAP acquired AppGyver and over the next 12 to 18 months, it was integrated into BTP, most notably to support destinations so applications could access SAP data. In May of 2022, we started our 100 Days of Low code series.

00;00;38;11 - 00;01;11;22
Daniel Wroblewski
We had a challenge to see what the community, what types of apps the community members would create from SAP Build Apps. And in November of that year, we had the opportunity to share the stage at TechEd, the first post Covid in person TechEd, and we shared it with the CTO to announce SAP Build. And so as a AppGyver became one of the tools of that Low-Code suite and its name was changed to SAP Build Apps.

00;01;11;24 - 00;01;43;16
Daniel Wroblewski
But alas, in the age of AI with SAP Build Apps was supplanted by other tools. My colleague Patrick Scheid has created a blog post that explains the decision to deprecate, build apps and makes four key points. First of all, all development. Now all app development. No can be done with SAP Build and the tools that are available there, for example, Build Code and to create CAP applications.

00;01;43;18 - 00;02;23;05
Daniel Wroblewski
But SAP Build Apps will continue to be supported for those who have purchased it through the end of the contract. Now, existing web or mobile apps that you created with SAP Build Apps, unfortunately cannot be migrated to other tools, and you will need to recreate those apps from scratch using Build Code or any other development tool. Now to migrate back end, entities that you created with visual cloud Functions, you can export the data to CSV files and then import it to, other backends.

00;02;23;08 - 00;02;42;26
Daniel Wroblewski
But you will have to create those backends and those entities in order to import is it's been a long, strange trip with SAP AppGyver. And then as they Build Apps and I'm happy to have met and helped so many of you on their journey.

00;02;42;28 - 00;03;10;17
Antonio Maradiaga
Hola! SAP Developers. Edge Integration Cell version 8.40 is now available and it's packed with features you've been asking for. First, the NFS adapter, sender, and receiver. You can now connect directly to a network file system service or FTP2 adapter for all your EDI and B2B file transfers. So if you're doing all that file transfers, with your trading partners, this is what you need.

00;03;10;19 - 00;03;33;10
Antonio Maradiaga
Edge Integration Cell also now supports an external cloud connector. You no longer need to use the cloud connector that's running in the cluster. Finally, Edge Integration Cell also supports bulk, meaning that you can now use it as a data store. All the documentation links are in the description below. Hasta luego!

00;03;33;12 - 00;03;54;05
Nora von Thenen
As SAP is part of the A2A protocol initiative. My colleague Pavel Conniff just released a really cool new tool to contribute to the A2A ecosystem. The tool is called the A2A editor, and he developed it as an open source project funded by the EU. You can think of the A2A editor as a swagger like interface, but for designing agent cards and testing and validating the agent's capabilities.

00;03;54;07 - 00;04;11;10
Nora von Thenen
So I have part of the playground, which is part of the A2A editor. You have a JSON editor, an agent card overview, a chat integration to test an Http inspector, and more. So make sure you check it out under the URL somewhere here and in the show notes. I actually just tried it out and I find it really cool.

00;04;11;13 - 00;04;30;18
Nora von Thenen
I'm probably going to include it into the AI agent, the code base agent, CodeJam that Kevin and I just recently developed and started to give a couple of weeks ago in Amsterdam, and now we will also be doing a couple of those CodeJams in Brazil as part of the AI Roadshow. Vitaly I don't know, do we call it that?

00;04;30;20 - 00;04;59;29
Nora von Thenen
If not, we start calling it that now. So in the middle of April, we're actually going to Brazil for a lot of CodeJams all around the topic of AI. There will be HANA machine learning, getting started with, AI core, and also the new code based AI agents CodeJam. And in April or something else is happening around AI, which is the developer challenge on the topic of AI, but more to the next week.

00;05;00;04 - 00;05;26;13
Kevin Kiedelsheimer
Did you know that you can use the orchestration client from the SAP Cloud SDK for AI? To create a full AI orchestration, flow in CAP. You can simply instantiate an orchestration client from the orchestration package within the SDK, and then define the configuration for your orchestration flow via JavaScript object Notation. The orchestration client allows you to use different foundation models without the need to change the client code.

00;05;26;15 - 00;05;40;17
Kevin Kiedelsheimer
It allows you to configure templating, content filtering, data masking, and much more. You can either write the configuration directly or download it from the SAP AI launchpad and use it from file, or directly copy it into your project.

entry.title: Centralized Error Handling in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Cloud Integration, runtime errors can occur due to various reasons such as script failures, adapter issues, or unexpected payloads. Handling such errors individually in every integration flow often leads to duplicated logic and increased maintenance effort.

This blog demonstrates a centralized error handling mechanism in SAP Cloud Integration that:

  • Captures errors using an Exception Subprocess
  • Forwards error data using the ProcessDirect adapter
  • Sends HTML-formatted email notifications
  • Is reusable across multiple business integration flows

Solution

The solution consists of two types of integration flows:

1. Business Integration Flows

  • Executes business logic
  • Uses an Exception Subprocess to capture errors
  • Sends error details to a centralized logger

2. Central Error Logger Integration Flows

  • Receives error messages from multiple iFlows
  • Normalizes error data
  • Sends styled HTML email notifications to the support team

Step 1: Create the Business Integration Flow

1. Navigate to the SAP Integration Suite

2. Go to Design → Integrations and APIs

3. Create a new package (example: PCK_CentralErrorLogging)

4. Create a new Integration Flow named Business_Test_Iflow

This iFlow represents a sample business process where a runtime error occurs.

 

Step 2: Configure the Sender and Main Process

1. Add an HTTPS Sender Adapter

  • Configure endpoint address (example: /testError)
  • Configure authentication as required

2. Connect the adapter to the Start Event

3. Add a Groovy Script named Script_Groovy_Generate_Error

  • This script deliberately throws an exception for testing purposes

4. Connect the script to the End Event

 

Step 3: Add Exception Subprocess for Error Capture

1. Add an Exception Subprocess to the Business iFlow

2. Add a Groovy Script inside the subprocess named Script_Groovy_CaptureError

The Groovy script performs the following responsibilities:

  • Capture integration flow name
  • Capture message ID
  • Generate correlation ID
  • Capture error message
  • Generate timestamps (IST and UTC)

All values are stored as message properties. Using an Exception Subprocess enables centralized error capture without modifying individual processing steps.

 

Step 4: Send Error Details via ProcessDirect Adapter

1. After the Groovy script, add a ProcessDirect Receiver Adapter

2. Configure the address as /centralErrorLogger

This enables reusability across multiple business iFlows.

Step 5: Create the Central Error Logger Integration Flow

1. Create an integration flow named Central_Error_Logger_Iflow

2. Configure the Sender Adapter as ProcessDirect

3. Use the same address /centralErrorLogger

Step 6: Normalize Incoming Error Data

1. Add a Groovy Script as the first step

2. The script reads error details from properties, headers, or payload and applies fallback values if data is missing, preparing a consistent structure for email formatting.

This makes the central logger reusable.

Step 7: Format Error Details into HTML

1. Add a Content Modifier

2. Create an HTML table displaying:

  • Integration Flow Name
  • Message ID
  • Adapter Type
  • Error Message
  • Timestamp (IST and UTC)
  • Correlation ID

 

Step 8: Configure Email Notification

1. Add a Mail Receiver Adapter

2. Configure mail server, sender and receiver email IDs, subject, and set MIME type to text/html

Step 9: End-to-End Testing

1. Deploy both integration flows

2. Trigger the business iFlow using the HTTP endpoint

3. Verify the following:

  • Business iFlow succeeds in Message Monitor
  • Central Error Logger receives the error
  • HTML email is delivered with error details

 

Validation Using Another Business Integration Flow

The same centralized error handling was validated with another real-world integration flow named Invoice_Batch_Email_Processor_With_ProcessDirect.

  • Multiple processing steps
  • Routers based on message severity
  • Multiple email notifications
  • Groovy scripts and content modifiers

 

Key Observations

  • Exception Subprocess captured runtime errors successfully
  • The same central error logger iFlow was reused
  • No changes were required in the logger
  • HTML email alerts were generated correctly

 

This confirms the solution works for both simple and complex integration flows.

Benefits of This Approach

  • Centralized error handling
  • Reusable across multiple iFlows
  • Clean separation of business logic and error handling
  • Faster troubleshooting using Message ID and Correlation ID
  • Professional HTML email notifications
  • Proven in real-world scenarios

Conclusion

By implementing centralized error handling using Exception Subprocess and ProcessDirect adapters, SAP Cloud Integration landscapes become easier to maintain, monitor, and support. This approach reduces duplication, improves observability, and scales efficiently across integration designs.

This solution was implemented and tested in SAP Integration Suite, and all screenshots are from real execution to ensure accuracy and practical relevance.

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt D: Improvements)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first mini-series: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass 😉. There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

This week, I re-did the Joule Setup on a Parallel Line of the SAP S/4HANA ERP Public Edition, aka Public Cloud. I noticed a few changes and would like to outline here for your reference:

You can install Joule on your Parallel Line

Several weeks ago, I met one of our customers at the Customer Success Day in NYC. After listening to his rational behind requiring Joule on the Parallel Line, I promised to do a test. I did just that this week on a Parallel Line. It worked.

No need to NavigationService

I heard there is an improvement in the Setup process, i.e., no more need to manually create the NavigationService destination (outlined in Step B.2.4 of my blog My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Pt B:Joule Installation). If you continue to add this destination, it won’t hurt because the system is no longer seeking the information stored there. I will remove this step in my blog.

Joule Booster is Faster

The Joule Booster is noticeably faster.  In my case, it is about 15 minutes to complete the running.

SAP Work Zone Content Provider

When I provision the user and group information from the Public Cloud to the Work Zone via IPS, I got an error: The provided providerId is illegal: "status":400,"scimType":"invalidValue".  There is even an KBA addressing this problem 3505749 - The provided providerId is illegal: "status":400,"scimType":"invalidValue".  It turns out not because of my ProviderID is bad, but I only copied part of the ProviderID 6d5e30cb-4 from sap.start.6d5e30cb-4.  I learned that from creating my own ProviderID.  The entire string is the ProviderID, not part of it.  The details count.

References

  1. Blog 1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  2. Blog 2: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  3. Discovery Center 1: Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  4. Discovery Center 2: Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  5. SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide
  6. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part A Prepara...
  7. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part B Joule I...
  8. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part C Joule D...
  9. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part D Improvements)

entry.title: Inside SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 2026: What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is almost here, and SAP Build at Sapphire is where customers and partners will be able to see how to easily create and deploy AI agents starting with simple language descriptions.  

For anyone tracking where AI agents and agentic development are headed in the enterprise, SAP Build will offer a chance to see it up close. Attendees will be able to experience how SAP Build helps teams build AI agents across SAP. This includes hands-on experience, live demos, sessions, and direct access to the teams building these capabilities. 

This year, the focus is on making agentic development real - moving from intent to working solution through a guided, end-to-end experience. 

 The hands-on experience: Build an agent with Joule Studio 

The biggest draw for SAP Build at Sapphire will be the hands-on experience on the show floor. 

Attendees can sit down at an open station and work through guided scenarios in a live system – building agents with Joule Studio, generating app logic from intent, and integrating with SAP data and workflows. This is a practical view at how agentic development works from start to deployment. 

This experience builds on the popular SAP Build Developer Garage at TechEd, which became a go-to stop for developers who wanted to get hands-on and see SAP Build in action. At Sapphire, we are bringing that energy back and making it bigger for a broader audience. 

Space will be limited, and access will be first-come, first-serve, so this is one stop worth making early. 

The sessions 

SAP Build sessions at Sapphire will focus on how teams are building AI agents, clean-core extensions and automation workflows. You’ll see how agentic development is evolving - from defining intent to generating and deploying working solutions - and how Joule is integrated across the development lifecycle. 

For anyone trying to separate AI hype from AI execution, these sessions are worth prioritizing. 

The booth 

The SAP Build and Agentic AI booths are where live walk-throughs and real conversations happen. 

Bring your own use cases, connect directly with SAP experts, and validate how SAP Build fits into your architecture. Whether you are building your first extension or scaling AI-driven workflows, the booth’s are designed for both strategic and practical discussions.  

Expect demos, conversations, and a chance to get closer to what SAP Build is showing at Sapphire. 

 

Why stop by SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 

AI agents are a major focus at Sapphire but SAP Build is where you can see how they are actually built and deployed in SAP environments. 

Get hands-on. Talk to the experts. See how agentic development works in practice. 

Plan your SAP Build stop at SAP Sapphire: 

  • Register for SAP Sapphire 
  • Browse SAP Build sessions 
  • Find the SAP Build booth (see the map onsite)
